---
title: "00 · Kiselev — Algebra, Part I"
subtitle: "Signed numbers, polynomials, factoring, fractions, equations and inequalities of the first degree, powers and roots, quadratic equations — the classical Russian Algebra opener."
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **Kiselev Algebra, Part I** track — A. P. Kiselev's
opener to school algebra, taught to generations of Russian students.
Part I builds from algebraic operations on signed integers up to
quadratic equations and the early theory of functions. Part II
([`ka2`](../tier-kiselev-2-algebra-2/index.qmd)) picks up at irrational
and complex numbers.

By the end of Part I you can:

- Operate with **positive and negative numbers**, treating *minus* as a
  legitimate quantity rather than a bookkeeping symbol.
- Multiply, divide, and factor **polynomials** with confidence.
- Add, multiply, and simplify **algebraic fractions**.
- Solve **linear equations and systems** in one and several unknowns.
- Solve **linear inequalities** and reason about ordering with signs.
- Manipulate **powers and roots** with both integer and fractional
  exponents.
- Solve **quadratic equations** by factoring, completing the square,
  and the formula — and recognise when each is the right tool.
- Read and write the early **language of functions and graphs**.

## Source

Russian original (public domain on archive.org):
[Алгебра. Ч.1, А. П. Киселёв](https://archive.org/details/1_kiselev_algebra)
— this track maps onto the chapter ordering of that scan. The PDF stays
external; the pointers in each lesson cite the relevant Russian sections.

A modern complete English translation by Valery Manokhin is available
(paid) on [Leanpub](https://leanpub.com/kiselevsalgebrapartone) — useful
if you'd rather read every word in English; this track works without it.

## Lessons

The full Algebra I track, in book order:

1. [**01 · Signed Numbers (Ch 1)**](01-signed-numbers/index.qmd) —
   positive and negative numbers and the four operations; the
   minus-times-minus-is-plus rule from distributivity.
2. [**02 · Polynomials (Ch 2)**](02-polynomials/index.qmd) —
   monomials, polynomials, the four operations; the three classical
   identities.
3. [**03 · Factoring (Ch 3)**](03-factoring/index.qmd) — common
   factor, identities, grouping, Vieta, the factor theorem.
4. [**04 · Algebraic Fractions (Ch 4)**](04-algebraic-fractions/index.qmd) —
   simplification by factoring; common denominator; complex
   fractions; telescoping.
5. [**05 · Linear Equations (Ch 5)**](05-linear-equations/index.qmd) —
   $ax + b = 0$ and the three cases by $a, b$; word problems.
6. [**06 · Linear Systems (Ch 6)**](06-linear-systems/index.qmd) —
   substitution, elimination, Cramer's rule; classification by
   determinant.
7. [**07 · Linear Inequalities (Ch 7)**](07-linear-inequalities/index.qmd) —
   the flip rule when multiplying by negatives; absolute-value
   inequalities; systems.
8. [**08 · Powers and Roots (Ch 8)**](08-powers-roots/index.qmd) —
   integer, zero, negative, fractional exponents; radicals;
   rationalising the denominator.
9. [**09 · Quadratic Equations (Ch 9)**](09-quadratic-equations/index.qmd) —
   factoring, completing the square, the quadratic formula, Vieta,
   discriminant analysis.

**Track complete.** Continue to
[*Algebra Part II*](../tier-kiselev-2-algebra-2/index.qmd) for
irrational and complex numbers, exponentials and logarithms,
sequences, combinatorics, and limits.

## Sister synthesis tier

The synthesis lessons covering the same ground sit in
[Tier 1 Foundations](../tier-1-foundations/index.qmd) (negative numbers,
intro to variables) and
[Tier 2 Pre-Algebra](../tier-2-prealgebra/index.qmd) (polynomials,
linear equations, factoring, square roots, quadratics). Each Kiselev
lesson links to its specific sister.
